{{Infobox sacred-art
| name = Lirial
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Lirial Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Lirial is a [[Territory]] characterized by its gray plains and clear skies, though it features twelve shifting moons that dictate the movement of subtle forces. It is not a combat-oriented realm; instead, its historical role involves serving as watchful sentinels and scouts for the [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]]. The powers associated with Lirial focus on protection, revelation, and the gathering and preservation of information rather than destruction. [[Travelers]] from this [[Territory]] utilize various crystal artifacts, such as discs, orbs, lenses, and teardrop-shaped recording stones, to observe distant events or communicate. A unique restriction for [[Lirial Travelers]] is that summoned objects, such as a crystal ball, must remain in their original location within a sanctum to be successfully called. [[Traveling]] through Lirial is a complex process that requires precise calculations based on the patterns of its twelve moons, and the location of Lirial [[Gates]] shifts according to these lunar movements. [[Travelers]] may also utilize a personal [[Source]], a crystal linked to their [[essence]] that stores and converts energy to power Lirial devices. Additionally, the name Lirial refers to an entity or persona that can manifest as a figure made of white crystal and silver wire, capable of opening [[Gates]] or utilizing light-based abilities.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] that reveals, protects, and preserves.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lirial]] has gray plains and a sky with a dozen moons that shift in patterns.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lirial]] is a power that [[Leah]] can call upon through her bracelet.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lirial]] is a place where the crystal disc resides.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Lirial is a [[Territory]] characterized by a clear sky and rare weather patterns, overseen by twelve shifting moons. This location serves as a place where power can be stored and summoned through the use of specific devices, and it is notably not a combat-oriented region. [[Leah's Source]] is situated within this territory, which can be accessed via a crystal bracelet. Despite the passage of time, Lirial remains a standing location.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a place where power can be stored and called upon via devices.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah's Source]] is located in [[Lirial]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] with twelve shifting moons.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] where weather is rare and the sky is usually clear.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is not a combat-oriented [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a location or entity that can be reached through a crystal bracelet.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]] is a location that still stands.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Lirial is a woman composed of white crystal and silver wire who manifests as a trio of stars whirling within a halo. As a territory containing the [[Crystal Fields]] and various desolate parts, she is an entity associated with the recording crystal and maintains a connection to [[Leah]]. Though unsuited for battle, this [[Incarnation]] displays both curiosity and resentment while examining a levitating javelin adorned with silver-white crystals. After [[Leah]] banished a scrying lens back to her, the [[Elysian Incarnation]] recruited seven [[Lirial Travelers]] and sent Lirial ahead to prepare the way. During a confrontation, Lirial raised her hands toward the far side of a building and utilized a star-net technique to attack [[Leah]] with white light and crystals.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] will likely find a [[library]] and start reading.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] containing the [[Crystal Fields]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is an entity associated with the recording crystal.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] banished her scrying lens back to [[Lirial]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Elysian Incarnation]] has recruited seven [[Lirial Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] was examining a levitating javelin with silver-white crystals.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] that is unsuited for battle.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is a [[Territory]] that [[Leah]] has a connection to.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is an [[Incarnation]] who shows curiosity and resentment.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] contains desolate parts.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is sent ahead to prepare the way.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is a woman of white crystal and silver wire.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] cannot be used to scout because there is a [[Lirial Incarnation]] on the enemy side.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] raised her hands toward the far side of the building.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] is a trio of stars whirling around in a halo.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] wore a dress of silver wire.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]] used a star-net technique to attack [[Leah]] with crystals and white light.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“I know. At least we’re sure Helgard and Lirial still stand.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Sacred Arts]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
